quoteFirst: Therefore it is possible that if on the day of Caesar’s landing high water occurred at Dover at 7.31 a.m., the tide may have turned westward as early as
quoteLast: , and may have continued to run westward for a period not longer than 5 hours, 53 minutes, that is to say, until 5.4 p.m.
title: Ancient Britain and the Invasions of Julius Caesar
author: Holmes, T. Rice (Thomas Rice), 1855-1933

quoteLast: on the eleventh you will whisper to the mail clerk: 'It is eleven-eleven, to-day the eleventh.
title: The Plunderers: A Novel
author: Lefevre, Edwin, 1871-1943; Bracker, M. Leone, 1885-1937 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: He told an absurd story of having been hired at the rate of one thousand dollars a week to ask in a whisper for eleven letters at the American Express Company's office on February 11th, at
quoteLast: , and declared that when his employer bade him good-by on the steamer he painted a scar on the back of his hand and told him always to wear black gloves.
title: The Plunderers: A Novel
author: Lefevre, Edwin, 1871-1943; Bracker, M. Leone, 1885-1937 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: It will be in apogee, or farthest from the earth, on the 13th, at 3:42 a.
title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, January 1885, No. 4
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]
